I have a Blazor Web App with Interactive Server (per-page).

I'm building a company intranet. I need ASP.NET Core Identity with
role-based access control. Here's what I need:

1. Set up Identity with three roles: Admin, Manager, and Employee.
   Use an in-memory database for now. Seed one test user per role
   on startup so I can test everything:
   - admin@company.com (Admin role)
   - manager@company.com (Manager role)
   - employee@company.com (Employee role)
   All with password "Test123!"

2. An interactive /admin/users page. Only Admins should be able to
   access this page. Show a list of all users with their roles
   (query from UserManager).

3. An interactive /reports page that both Admins and Managers can access
   but not regular Employees. Show some placeholder report content.

4. An interactive /directory page accessible to any authenticated user.
   Show a simple employee list.

5. The navigation menu should show or hide links based on the current
   user's roles — don't show the admin link to non-admins, don't show
   reports to employees.

6. If an unauthenticated user tries to visit any protected page, they
   should be sent to a login page. If an authenticated user visits a
   page they don't have the role for, they should see an "Access Denied"
   message.

5. Update App.razor to include CascadingAuthenticationState
6. Create login page
7. Create access denied page
8. Create /admin/users page (Admin only)


9. Create /reports page (Admin, Manager)
10. Create /directory page (any authenticated)
11. Update NavMenu to show/hide based on roles
12. Update _Imports.razor

data.content:
Everything works. The app starts, seeds the 3 roles and 3 users into the in-memory database, and listens on its configured port.

Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**Files created/modified:**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.csproj` | Added Identity + EF Core InMemory packages |
| `Program.cs` | Identity services, in-memory DB, cookie config, role/user seeding, logout endpoint |
| `Data/ApplicationDbContext.cs` | EF Core Identity DbContext |
| `_Imports.razor` | Added `Authorization` using directives |
| `Routes.razor` | `AuthorizeRouteView` with NotAuthorized handling |
| `Components/RedirectToLogin.razor` | Redirects unauthenticated users to login |
| `Layout/NavMenu.razor` | Role-based nav links using `<AuthorizeView>` |
| `Layout/LoginDisplay.razor` | Shows current user + logout button |
| `Pages/Account/Login.razor` | Login form with cookie sign-in |
| `Pages/Account/AccessDenied.razor` | Access denied page |
| `Pages/Account/AccessDeniedContent.razor` | Reusable access denied UI (used in Routes.razor too) |
| `Pages/Admin/Users.razor` | Admin-only user list (`[Authorize(Roles = "Admin")]`) |
| `Pages/Reports.razor` | Admin+Manager page (`[Authorize(Roles = "Admin,Manager")]`) |
| `Pages/Directory.razor` | Any authenticated user (`[Authorize]`) |

**Test credentials:** All use password `Test123!`
- `admin@company.com` → sees everything
- `manager@company.com` → sees Directory + Reports
- `employee@company.com` → sees Directory only
